paragraphs.body: ​ Have you ever heard a phrase or question so deep that it leaves you questioning your own reality? Well that happens alot in the movie the matrix and if you really pay attention it’s shocking how similar our worlds really are. Lana and Lilly Wachowski are the directors of this film but I don’t think even they knew how close their fictional world is to becoming a reality. There is one person who understood these messages deeper than anyone else, Wendy Liu the author of a critical analysis of the matrix.

annotations.text: Wendy Liu the author of a critical analysis of the matrix.
annotations.comment: this suggests to me that you'll be summarizing her essay? Is the next paragraph the summary? If so, try using the summary templates/moves to clarify who and what you're summarizing :)

paragraphs.body: ​ The movie starts off with a hacker Neo who gets a feeling like something is wrong with the world but he just doesn't know what, he feels a feeling that nothing around him seems real. Neo begins to get a text from Morpheous asking him if he wants to discover the truth. This alone already draws many comparisons with humans as is, our nature to always want to know why everything works and what’s really going on can be our strength but also our biggest weakness. Morpheus begins to talk to Neo and tell him “the world you see is not real but simply a jail but not just a physical jail no it’s deeper than that it’s a jail for your mind”, and the only way you can free yourself from it is to first realize it’s there. This statement alone draws many comparisons with the real world and really America itself, how we're given the illusion of choice on predetermined outcomes, outcomes that we are deceived into choosing by the ones who have made the very matrix itself. He also describes the matrix as “a jail you can neither see nor touch”. This jail is fitting more and more of the traits of socialization. What socialization is, is controlled behavior through taught behavior essentially it’s point is basically you can get humans to think anything is normal and be ok with it through making the “common public” agree, if you think I’m wrong look at our history things like slavery, domestic abuse, rape, and many more violent acts used to be seen as the normal is past society and now in this one it’s an egregious crime.

annotations.text: This alone already draws many comparisons with humans as is, our nature to always want to know why everything works and what’s really going on can be our strength but also our biggest weakness.
annotations.comment: Do you feel this way about your own life?
You've repeated the idea of "jail" several times, which suggests that it's important to you. Let's talk about how you might view the world as a kind of "jail" in your own experience :)

paragraphs.body: Morpheus then offers Neo two choices: on one hand take the blue pill, with the blue pill he’ll wake right back up not realizing anything has happened, then his other option is to take the red pill and he’ll see how deep this “rabbit hole” really goes. This choice right here is showing human nature, you see most people would take the blue pill and go to sleep without knowing because many more humans would rather live in a false reality and be unaware of it then to actually try to address the problems in the current world. You can draw parallels to drug addicts and how they take all of these harmful drugs because if they stop then they have to leave their fantasy world where they have no real world problems. Neo naturally takes the red pill bringing out his human curiosity and when he takes it he wakes up in the real world, turns out the world he thought he was living in was no more than a simulation and in the real world civilizations was destroyed and all humans were in pods run by AI simply used as nourishment for the AI’s to exist. This becomes awfully real considering the movie was made in 1999 now it’s 2025 and we have an uprising of AI and other tech like it that exist to make human life easier.

annotations.text: have an uprising of AI and other tech like it that exist to make human life easier.
annotations.comment: is this something that worries you? Let's explore!

This is still summary and report. How much of this summary do you need to "point" your reader to your own essay? Remember that your life, your experience, your ideas are the most important ones for this essay.
annotations.context_left: t’s 2025 and we have an uprising of AI and other tech like it that exist to make human life easier.

paragraphs.body: In the movie Neo has to learn how to fight the AI’s and in order to do that he has to go back inside the matrix to and realize that, that world isn’t bound by physical limitations but only mental ones allowing him to be able to dodge bullets, jump out of planes, and even fly, see now obviously you can’t do any of these in the real world but it’s a metaphor for all the unlimited possibilities humans are capable of if they believe they can do it. Morpheus tells Neo the doubt he has in his mind that things are impossible you’re conditioned to feel that way by the Matrix to keep you where they want you to be to make sure you’re an obedient slave. If you haven’t guessed by now this is dead accurate to our world now we live in a society where we are trained by the so called “matrix” and only taught to do a certain thing to simply be useful to the one’s at the top in control of it all. To go further on how deep this really is kids in high crime areas, all they know is what their environment is or the matrix so to speak they’ve never seen anyone try to become a business man they’ve never had these ideas in their head and if they did they were quickly shot down by the matrix itself with the requirements it takes to be in those positions. So naturally they’ll turn to crime too and it’s a never ending cycle you see how this relates to the movie? These people are in jail not a physical one but a mental one, right from birth they’re conditioned and taught that this is what they must do.

annotations.text: If you haven’t guessed by now this is dead accurate to our world now we live in a society where we are trained by the so called “matrix” and only taught to do a certain thing to simply be useful to the one’s at the top in control of it all.

paragraphs.body: To go deeper than that, that’s just one example the matrix applies to everyone showing that you’ll always be a product of your environment unless you make the conscious choice to realize you’re in the matrix and be different the system is set up that way to keep you obedient and feed the pockets of the one’s in control just like the AIs. The matrix is so deep now it’s in everything we do per the article, as a young kid Americans are put in elementary school usually last around 8 hours, then they’re put in middle school last around 8 hours, and then high school at the same timeframe. This is conditioning your mind to work it’s best during these times and building its own matrix so when it’s time to choose a job you know what most Americans have a 9-5 because subconsciously they’ve been subjected to these hours since kids and it being all they know continuing the cycle of the jail you can’t see or touch because these jobs are good but ultimately these are jobs that really just help the elite the top 1% of people because as long as they have workers and nobody thinks “I want to be the owner not the worker” they got you right where they want you to forever keep them malnourished just like the AI’s in the film.

annotations.text: The matrix is so deep now it’s in everything we do per the article, as a young kid Americans are put in elementary school usually last around 8 hours, then they’re put in middle school last around 8 hours, and then high school at the same timeframe.
